title: Implement Tier C authorization gate + singleton approval record (UTV2-1451 child)

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Local pre-push head at proof-authoring time. PR head SHA and merge SHA to be bound post-push/merge per standard proof-SHA-binding automation (post-merge-lane-close.yml runs ops:proof-generate --merge-sha).

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Not independent Griff review, t1-approved, or a pm-verdict/v1 owner artifact.

hook_integration_proof.note: Invokes the hook binary directly with a manifest-authorized PreToolUse stdin payload, captures real stdout, asserts valid JSON with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision/additionalContext + top-level systemMessage present and non-empty. Also asserts the un-authorized-block (exit 2) and non-Tier-C-passthrough (exit 0, no output) paths are unchanged. This is the acceptance-criteria integration test / captured-behavior proof, not a design assertion.

full_test_suite.note: Includes test:ops (1135 tests, 0 fail, includes the 37 new tests above) and the new test:hooks script (11/11), plus every pre-existing suite.

r_level.note: Not run standalone as a separate command; pnpm verify's full green run covers the same gates. No R-level artifacts required for this diff (pure hook/CI/ops-script change, no lifecycle/settlement/promotion path touched).

runtime_proof.note: This lane changes only a local Claude Code hook, CI gate scripts, a schema doc, and a pure Linear-GraphQL validator module; no production code path or migration touched. Live smoke run (apps/api/src/database-smoke.test.ts) regardless per T1 policy, same reasoning as UTV2-1550's proof.

queries.description: INSERT via processSubmission — one row per synthetic pick, in the 6 of 7 tests that create a pick (all except the read-only UTV2-883 participants test)
INSERT via processSubmission (same 6 tests); UPDATE lifecycle_state via transitionPickLifecycle (queued, posted) and via the settlement RPC path

INSERT on each successful transitionPickLifecycle call and on each successful settlement transition; SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests (UTV2-920 invalid enqueue/delivery/settlement) to assert zero rows were written for a specific to_state after a rejected atomic operation

INSERT via one successful enqueueDistributionAtomic call (UTV2-920 invalid-delivery test setup); UPDATE status to 'processing' via claimNextAtomic; a separate enqueue attempt (UTV2-920 invalid-enqueue test) is rejected and asserted via findByIdempotencyKey returning null
SELECT COUNT(*) used to assert zero rows were written after a rejected confirmDeliveryAtomic call — no receipt row is created anywhere in this test run

INSERT via recordPickSettlement/settlePickAtomic on successful calls; one settlePickAtomic call (UTV2-920 invalid settlement) is rejected and asserted via SELECT COUNT(*) returning zero; SELECT used to assert exactly one base record (corrects_id null) and that a correction does not mutate the original row

SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests to assert zero audit rows for a rejected atomic operation (enqueue/delivery/settlement), and by one test to assert at least two audit rows exist for a successful settlement plus its correction
read-only SELECT (listByType('player')) scanning the full live table for the UTV2-883 duplicate (external_id, sport, participant_type) invariant — no writes

linear_graphql_verification.note: Introspected Linear's live GraphQL schema (not assumed from docs, which do not document these fields) to confirm Issue.creator and Comment.{id,url,body,user,botActor} field names before designing singleton-approval.ts. Queried the real UTV2-1570 issue and its own comments to confirm the actual comment URL format and discover that the #comment-<id> URL fragment is a truncated 8-character prefix of the full comment UUID, not the full ID -- this is why the validator matches by exact url equality against the comment's own canonical field rather than reconstructing an ID from the ref string.
queries_run: introspection: __type(name: "Comment") { fields { name type { name kind } } } — confirmed id/url/body/user/botActor fields

live query: issue(id: "UTV2-1570") { creator { id name email } comments(first: 10) { nodes { id url body user { id name email } botActor { id } } } } — confirmed real URL format and truncated fragment

followup_paths: scripts/ops/lane-start.ts (wire --singleton-approval-ref into the existing flag check; blocked by concurrent UTV2-1569 file-scope lock at authoring time)
scripts/ops/lane-start.test.ts (new/updated cases for the wired-in validator)

acceptance_criteria.singleton_approval_ref_replaces_bare_fla: PARTIAL — validator built and tested; lane-start.ts wiring deferred (documented gap, see known_gaps)
acceptance_criteria.singleton_approval_author_matched_agains: PASS (live Linear GraphQL query, not a config allowlist)
acceptance_criteria.legacy_flag_deprecation_warning_not_suff: DESIGNED_IN_SCHEMA_DOC — not yet enforced at the lane-start.ts call site (same wiring gap)
acceptance_criteria.file_scope_declared_before_implementatio: PASS (declared via lane manifest before implementation; extended once for the singleton-approval-v1.md schema doc, per the same before-not-after discipline)

known_gaps: scripts/ops/lane-start.ts is NOT wired to call the new singleton-approval.ts validator in this PR. That file (and scripts/ops/lane-start.test.ts) are currently held by another active, unrelated concurrent T1 lane's declared file_scope_lock (UTV2-1569's Fable-pilot-routing lane, PR #1292, still open, mergeStateStatus BLOCKED, awaiting its own PM review as of this writing). Touching either file here would create a hard file-scope-lock conflict, mechanically enforced by both ops:lane-start's own concurrency check at lane-creation time and CI's file-scope-lock-check.yml at PR-diff time -- forcing it through would either break my own lane-start invocation or fail CI on one of the two concurrent PRs. This means the bare --singleton-approved flag remains the only mechanism actually reachable from lane-start.ts today: the singleton self-authorization loophole is NOT yet mechanically closed end-to-end, only the validator half of it (the validator module itself, its Linear-comment schema, and its full 19-test suite are complete, tested, and ready to wire in). The wiring is a small, mechanical change (replace the current `flags.has('singleton-approved') || bools.has('singleton-approved')` check with a call into validateSingletonApprovalRef, emit a deprecation warning for the bare flag, fail closed with a stable error code) once the conflicting lane merges or closes. Recommend a fast, narrow follow-up lane once UTV2-1569 clears.

The Tier C Authorization Gate workflow is registered as a required-check CANDIDATE in REQUIRED_CI_CHECKS.md (§5.6), not yet flipped live in GitHub branch protection -- same rollout pattern used for Proof Auditor Gate (candidate first, promoted to required after a confirmed green run on a Tier-C-touching PR). Live branch protection today lists only: verify, Executor Result Validation, Merge Gate, P0 Protocol (confirmed via `gh api repos/.../branches/main/protection` at authoring time) -- several other 'always_required' entries already in REQUIRED_CI_CHECKS.md (e.g. proof-regression) are also not live-configured, so this gap is consistent with existing repo practice, not a new shortfall introduced by this lane. Promoting it to a live required check is a deliberate follow-up action, not something this PR performs unilaterally.

The bootstrap fallback in tier-c-authorization-gate.yml (falling back to this PR's own copy of tier-c-authorization-gate.ts / tier-c-approval-comment-parser.ts when origin/main has neither yet) is exercised on THIS PR's own first CI run by construction -- expected and self-resolving once this PR merges, same pattern file-scope-lock-check.yml used for file-scope-guard.ts under UTV2-1521.

round_1.finding: P2: issue_comment-triggered re-evaluation (posting TIER_C_APPROVAL after an earlier failing pull_request run) never created or updated a Checks API run bound to the PR's head_sha -- only the job's own native per-trigger status was produced, which branch protection would not associate with the PR's head SHA for the pull_request-triggered failure. A later valid approval comment would not actually unblock the PR without a manual rerun or new push.
round_1.fix: Restructured the workflow to explicitly create a Checks API run (checks.create) bound to the PR's headSha at the start of every evaluation (regardless of trigger event), and explicitly complete it (checks.update) with the correct conclusion at the end -- mirroring merge-gate.yml's own established checks.create/checks.update pattern exactly. The 'Evaluate Tier C authorization' step now uses continue-on-error so the completion step always runs and the explicit check never gets stuck in_progress; core.setFailed() still marks the job itself failed on a BLOCKED verdict for Actions-tab visibility.
